result:
Compile is green.

## Report

**Root cause:** `cg_infix` at `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:1286` only called `cg_discharge_vars` on the LHS of arithmetic and comparison binops. For LHS variants that emit instructions with a placeholder destination register (`UpVal`→`GetUpVal`, `Indexed`→`GetTable`, etc.), discharge marks the expression as `Reloc` but does *not* reserve a register. When the RHS was then parsed and discharged its own `Reloc` (e.g. `lim` as an upvalue in `lim+0.3`), `cg_exp_to_next_reg` picked the *same* register the LHS was waiting to be patched into. At runtime the second instruction overwrote the LHS value before the `EQ` ran. Symptom: `more[16] == lim+0.3` returned `false` even though both sides were `20.3`.

**Fix:** In `cg_infix`, for `Add/Sub/Mul/Div/IDiv/Mod/Pow/BAnd/BOr/BXor/Shl/Shr` and `Eq/Ne/Lt/Le/Gt/Ge`, call `cg_exp_to_any_reg` (which reserves a fresh register) for non-numeral LHS — matching C's `luaK_exp2anyreg` / `luaK_exp2RK` in `lcode.c`'s `luaK_infix`. Numeral constants (`KInt`/`KFlt`) still pass through `cg_discharge_vars` (no-op) so they remain available for any constant fold path.

**New state:** Original assertion at vararg.lua:58 (combined line 67) is gone. The test now advances much further and hits a separate, pre-existing Phase-B stub at `load_buffer_ex` (vararg.lua:102 `load[[...]]`) — that's a distinct missing piece, not a regression of this bug.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/min_vararg.lua << 'EOF'
local lim = 20
local i, a = 1, {}
while i <= lim do a[i] = i+0.3; i=i+1 end

local call = function (f, args) return f(table.unpack(args, 1, args.n)) end

function f(a, b, c, d, ...)
  local more = {...}
  print("a=", a, "b=", b, "c=", c, "d=", d)
  print("#more=", #more, "more[1]=", more[1], "more[16]=", more[16], "more[17]=", more[17])
  print("a==1.3", a == 1.3)
  print("more[1]==5.3", more[1] == 5.3)
  print("more[16]==20.3", more[16] == 20.3)
  print("more[17]==nil", more[17] == nil)
end

call(f, a)
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/min_vararg.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -30

tool_input.description: Run minimal vararg test via lua-rs

tool_input.command: SRC=$(/usr/bin/awk '{printf "%s\\n", $0}' /tmp/min_vararg.lua); echo "Source has $(wc -l < /tmp/min_vararg.lua) lines"; LUA_RS_BIN=target/debug/lua-rs; $LUA_RS_BIN "$(/usr/bin/awk 1 /tmp/min_vararg.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -30
